subject: Human Resources
title: The WARN Act
description: A quick, practical guide to The WARN Act.

hook.text: 900 employees arrive at work to find the doors locked. No warning. No notice. The WARN Act was designed to prevent exactly this.

buildup.text: The Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act requires 60 days' notice before mass layoffs or plant closings.

discovery.text: It applies to employers with 100+ employees laying off 50+ workers at a single site—or closing a facility entirely.

twist.text: Companies that violate WARN owe 60 days' back pay and benefits per affected employee—making surprise layoffs extremely costly.

climax.text: Some states have 'mini-WARN' acts with stricter requirements—lower thresholds, longer notice periods, or broader coverage.

punchline.text: Sixty days' notice gives people time to plan. Surprise layoffs take that away.

quiz.question: What does the WARN Act require?
options: 60 days' notice before mass layoffs or plant closings
Immediate severance pay for all employees
